architecture_fit.note: Single attention head, no MLP, hand-built QK circuit that solves the shift exactly; minimal delta but uses one-hot positional embeddings rather than base_model's RoPE.

baseline_comparison.note: Compares against the task-provided uniform 1/L baseline only; no constructed strawman (e.g. no-shift head or random logits) shown failing where the method succeeds.

faithfulness.note: Mechanism is the model_fn so it is faithful by construction, but no ablation is run and the README never flags it as synthetic nor proposes a causal check.

operating_range.note: Perfect and identical across k in {1,2,4,8,16} (~1.2 orders), but values are constant by construction and no edge-breaking regime is probed.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: The entire mechanism is an analytically hand-set QK circuit reproducing the target behaviour exactly — exactly what this bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Two coordinated panels (target mass vs uniform bars; accuracy & lift vs log2 k) make the claim legible, though lines are flat and a dead make_heatmap stub lingers.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies axes, the sweep on x, the two y-metrics and the anchored uniform baseline, directly tying the chart to the goal's 'does it hold across k' question.

notes: A clean, correct hand-built QK circuit that solves relative positional shift exactly across the full k sweep, earning top marks on the hardcoded-weights bonus and good architecture fit. It is weaker on the testable claims: the only baseline is the built-in uniform floor, no causal ablation or synthetic-caveat is offered, and operating range is constant-by-construction with no breaking regime explored. Solid mechanistic demonstration that stops short of the comparative and causal rigor the rubric prioritizes.
